story.iWant: comprehensive tenant isolation tests validating all 3 layers
story.soThat: I can prove multi-tenancy security is bulletproof
story.tasks: - AC1: TenantIsolationIntegrationTest.kt validates all 3 layers - AC2: Test scenarios - Layer 1: Missing tenant_id claim → 400 Bad Request - AC2: Test scenarios - Layer 2: Command tenant mismatch → TenantIsolationException - AC2: Test scenarios - Layer 3: Direct SQL query bypassing app → RLS blocks access - AC3: Cross-tenant attack scenarios tested (JWT with wrong tenant_id) - AC4: Test uses multiple Keycloak users with different tenant_id claims - AC5: All isolation tests pass - AC6: Test execution time <3 minutes - AC7: Test documented as security validation reference

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. TenantIsolationIntegrationTest.kt validates all 3 layers 2. Test scenarios: - Layer 1: Missing tenant_id claim → 400 Bad Request - Layer 2: Command tenant mismatch → TenantIsolationException - Layer 3: Direct SQL query bypassing app → RLS blocks access 3. Cross-tenant attack scenarios tested (JWT with wrong tenant_id) 4. Test uses multiple Keycloak users with different tenant_id claims 5. All isolation tests pass 6. Test execution time <3 minutes 7. Test documented as security validation reference

doc.relevance: Comprehensive testing strategy for defense-in-depth multi-tenancy
doc.snippet: 3-Layer Tenant Isolation Testing: - Layer 1 Test: Missing tenant_id claim in JWT → 400 Bad Request (fail-closed) - Layer 2 Test: Command tenant mismatch → TenantIsolationException (service validation) - Layer 3 Test: Direct SQL bypassing application → RLS blocks access (database enforcement) - Attack Scenarios: JWT with manipulated tenant_id, cross-tenant command injection - Performance Target: Full test suite <3 minutes execution time - Security Reference: Test suite serves as validation for OWASP compliance

Epic 3 Story 3.12: Security Fuzz Testing with Jazzer - 3 fuzz targets: JwtFormatFuzzer, TokenExtractorFuzzer, RoleNormalizationFuzzer - Comprehensive attack vector coverage (SQL, XSS, JNDI, Unicode, null bytes, CRLF) - 9 corpus seed files + auto-persistence - CI/CD integration (nightly pipeline) Pattern: Systematic security validation with attack scenarios

doc.relevance: Security compliance requirements for tenant isolation
doc.snippet: NFR002: Security compliance with OWASP ASVS Level 2 (50% target). Multi-tenancy security is highest-risk area requiring comprehensive validation. Defense-in-depth approach with independent layer testing. Security validation serves as compliance evidence.

Story 4.7: Tenant Isolation Integration Test Suite - TenantIsolationIntegrationTest.kt validates all 3 layers - Cross-tenant attack scenarios tested (JWT with wrong tenant_id) - Test uses multiple Keycloak users with different tenant_id claims - Test execution time <3 minutes - Test documented as security validation reference

artifact.reason: Story 4.6 multi-tenant Widget tests serve as foundation for comprehensive isolation tests
Epic 3 Keycloak test base for multi-user JWT generation
Epic 3 validation test patterns for attack scenarios
Epic 3 RBAC test patterns for authorization scenarios

dependency.name: PostgreSQL JDBC
dependency.purpose: Direct JDBC for Layer 3 RLS testing

constraints.constraint: Test each layer INDEPENDENTLY to validate defense-in-depth. Layer 1 failure should not prevent Layer 2/3 testing. Each layer must enforce tenant isolation even if other layers fail.

Test realistic attack scenarios: - JWT with manipulated tenant_id claim (Layer 1 bypass attempt) - Command with mismatched tenantId field (Layer 2 validation) - Direct SQL injection bypassing application (Layer 3 RLS enforcement) - Cross-tenant aggregate modification attempts - Missing tenant context at each layer (fail-closed validation)

Use Keycloak Testcontainers with multiple users: - User 1: tenant_id = "tenant-a" (realm role: widget_user) - User 2: tenant_id = "tenant-b" (realm role: widget_user) - Generate JWTs for both users, validate isolation between tenants
Full test suite execution <3 minutes (acceptance criteria). Optimize with: parallel test execution, test data reuse, connection pooling. Balance comprehensive coverage with fast feedback.
Test suite serves as security validation reference. Document test scenarios, attack patterns, and validation results. Include in security compliance evidence for OWASP ASVS.

interface.name: Layer 1: TenantContextFilter Validation
interface.description: Test servlet filter extracts tenant_id and rejects missing claims. Validate fail-closed behavior at HTTP request boundary.
interface.scenarios: - Missing tenant_id claim in JWT → 400 Bad Request - Valid tenant_id claim → TenantContext populated - Invalid JWT format → filter bypassed (Layer 1 assumes valid JWT from Epic 3)

interface.name: Layer 2: Axon Command Interceptor Validation
interface.description: Test Axon interceptor validates tenant context matches command. Validate service-level enforcement prevents cross-tenant commands.
interface.scenarios: - Command.tenantId != TenantContext → TenantIsolationException - Command.tenantId == TenantContext → command proceeds - Missing TenantContext → TenantIsolationException (fail-closed) - Tenant A attempts to modify Tenant B aggregate → rejected

interface.name: Layer 3: PostgreSQL RLS Validation
interface.description: Test PostgreSQL RLS blocks cross-tenant queries via direct SQL. Validate database-level enforcement even bypassing application.

interface.name: Cross-Tenant Attack Scenarios
interface.description: Test realistic attack scenarios for tenant isolation violations. Validate defense-in-depth prevents attacks at multiple layers.
interface.scenarios: - JWT tenant_id manipulation: Generate JWT for tenant-a, modify claim to tenant-b → Layer 1 rejects (invalid signature) - Command injection: Tenant-a user sends CreateWidgetCommand with tenantId="tenant-b" → Layer 2 rejects (context mismatch) - SQL injection: Bypass app layer with direct SQL → Layer 3 RLS blocks (database enforcement) - Aggregate hijacking: Tenant-a attempts PublishWidgetCommand on tenant-b widget → Layer 2 rejects

standards.standard: Constitutional TDD: Test suite serves as validation for Stories 4.1-4.6. Comprehensive integration tests validate end-to-end tenant isolation.
Use Kotest FunSpec with @SpringBootTest for full integration testing. Pattern: @Autowired field injection + init block (Epic 3 pattern). Use @ActiveProfiles("test") for test-specific configuration.
Document test suite as security validation reference. Include test scenarios in security compliance documentation. Test results serve as evidence for OWASP ASVS compliance.

ideas.idea: Test AC2 Layer 1: Missing tenant_id claim in JWT → 400 Bad Request
Test AC2 Layer 1: Valid tenant_id claim → TenantContext populated correctly
Test AC2 Layer 2: Command.tenantId != TenantContext → TenantIsolationException
Test AC2 Layer 2: Tenant A cannot modify Tenant B aggregate
Test AC2 Layer 3: Direct SQL query as Tenant A → cannot see Tenant B data (RLS)
Test AC2 Layer 3: RLS blocks cross-tenant UPDATE/DELETE operations
Test AC3: Cross-tenant attack - JWT tenant_id manipulation attempt
Test AC3: Cross-tenant attack - Command injection with wrong tenantId
Test AC3: Cross-tenant attack - SQL injection bypassing application
Test AC4: Multiple Keycloak users (tenant-a, tenant-b) with different JWTs
Test AC6: Full test suite execution time <3 minutes (performance validation)
Test all 3 layers in single scenario: Create widget as tenant-a, attempt access as tenant-b → blocked at all layers
Test fail-closed behavior: Missing TenantContext at each layer → reject request
